items.title: Not moving jobs, states or starting businesses: Data says Australians are stuck

children.content: People are less mobile, less dynamic and more risk-averse than we used to be, a series of economic indicators shows.

items.title: Trump's deal making leaves Iran in the driver's seat and Israel more vulnerable

children.content: Israel goes from looking like the new regional hegemon to looking like it is under greater threat now than it has in years, while Iran now has room to rebuild its proxies.

image.altText: Trump purses his lips as he walks outside the White House

items.title: The migration maths behind the housing crisis is not what politicians claim

children.content: Arthur Calwell argued migration was essential to national survival. Today, the Coalition says it should depend on how many homes get built, but the economics are far from straightforward.

image.altText: a male politician giving a speech in a chamber

items.title: The link between terrorism and DV could be key to preventing homicide

children.content: A world-first research project is aiming to prevent family and intimate partner homicides with similar strategies to those being used to tackle terrorism and lone-actor attacks.

items.title: Why global conflict is reshaping Australian politics

children.content: US President Donald Trump was yesterday touting an end to the war in Iran, but declaring mission accomplished in Washington will not spare Australian consumers, and politicians, from the economic fallout of the conflict.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson wearing a red suit jacket, speaking at a media conference.

items.title: Pocock open to party of independents as One Nation support climbs

children.content: The independent senator says Australians are frustrated with a political system that "does not work for them".

image.altText: A man in a shirt sits in a chair on a tv set talking to the host.

items.title: Head of Victoria's new childcare watchdog vows to protect kids

children.content: Former Victoria Police deputy commissioner Wendy Steendam assures parents the childcare system is safe, vowing to safeguard children following multiple cases of alleged abuse.

items.title: The once-promising AFL player hitting home runs in US Major League

children.content: As a teenager, Curtis Mead had to choose between an iconically Australian sport and America's pastime. He's now hitting home runs in Major League Baseball.

has died following a shark attack in Far North Queensland.

items.title: Politics and faith collide over choice for Tibet's next Dalai Lama

children.content: Tibetan Buddhists believe the Dalai Lama decides when and where he will reincarnate. But China's ruling Communist Party says it must choose who will replace him.

items.title: Scientist 'shocked' $300m irrigation scheme pushing ahead without studies

children.content: A $300 million irrigation scheme will supply 37.2 gigalitres of water to farming areas near Hobart from 2030. A scientists fears the taxpayer-funded scheme could put a major river at risk.

image.altText: She stands by the river with a red silky scarf around her neck

children.content: The raid marks the end of a three-day stand-off between members of the opposition CHP party and police.

items.title: My dog Teddy was more than just a pet. We needed each other

children.content: Judith Geppert has an impressive resume of achievements and celebrations, but her capabilities are often questioned. Her dog Teddy, however, never treated her any differently.

items.title: Trump skips son's wedding due to his 'love of the United States'

items.title: Aussie the first winner at French Open after injury retirement

children.content: James Duckworth gets an easy path into the second round of the French Open after his opponent retires midway through the second set.

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

image.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

items.title: This private island paradise has a bargain price tag, and problems galore

children.content: Makri Island, not far from mainland Greece, boasts almost 100 hectares of uninhabited Mediterranean beauty, but buyers be warned, this slice of paradise comes with a catch.

items.title: Fifty years ago, a killer returned the knife to the kitchen and slipped away

children.content: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.
